Held – the Petitioner is entitled to obtain writs as sought.

description: Gnei Wazeela Ramdeen Vs. Mass Milhan Sadar before Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: Gnei Wazeela Ramdeen Vs. Mass Milhan Sadar
judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner had filed an application to obtain a Faskh divorce before the Quazi Court of Gampaha. However, later having withdrawn that application, she had changed her residence to Panadura, where she had instituted a similar application. The Respondent had raised objections to the maintainability because she had recently come to reside within the jurisdiction of the Panadura Quazi Court; objection overruled, and proceeded with the divorce. Later, the Respondent, without notice, obtained a stay order from the Board of Quazi, and thereafter notified the Petitioner. Since there is a procedural error made by the Board of Quazi by not hearing the Petitioner, Court granted relief as prayed for
legistation: Muslim Marriage and Divorce Act

keywords: The Petitioner is an industrialist who was given a parcel of land in the Noorani Industrial Park under the purview of BOI to invest and develop the land and to carry on the business of the relevant industry for the purpose of achieving the objectives of the Industrial Promotion Act. The land so given under a long-term lease under the State Lands Ordinance. However, since 2013, the Petitioner ceased to operate the industry, and from 2016, with permission, had sub- leased the premises to the 8th Respondent for a higher rental; making a profit out of the sub-lease. Thereupon, the 4th to 6th Respondents decided to cancel the lease. The Petitioner challenged this decision in this Application; unsuccessful, due to non-fulfilment of the conditions in the lease
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka

description: Galle Pathirannehalage Ranjith Anuradha Pathirana Vs. The Commissioner of Title Settlement and Others before Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: Galle Pathirannehalage Ranjith Anuradha Pathirana Vs. The Commissioner of Title Settlement and Others
judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner sought to have his title registered in respect of a land already acquired by the Government. In fact, he has become the owner after Section 2 notice had been issued, and steps under Section 5 had been taken to identify the land; the land so identified belonged to the 5th Respondent, who had been identified to pay compensation. Therefore, in registering a land under the Title Registration Act, which had already been acquired and identified as a land not claimed by the Petitioner, and the Petitioner’s failure to establish a nexus between his land and the land claimed by the 5th Respondent, the Petitioner’s Application was dismissed.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka
